subject: Geography
title: Eratosthenes Measures the Earth
description: Learn Eratosthenes Measures the Earth in one minute.

hook.text: In 240 BCE, a Greek librarian calculated Earth's circumference using shadows and a stick.

buildup.text: Eratosthenes noticed the sun cast no shadow at noon in Syene but did in Alexandria.

discovery.text: Using the angle and distance between the cities, he calculated 40,000 km—almost exactly right.

twist.text: Columbus later used a smaller estimate and thought Asia was much closer than it was.

climax.text: If Columbus had trusted Eratosthenes, he would never have sailed west.

punchline.text: One stick, one shadow, one correct answer—2,200 years ago.

quiz.question: How did Eratosthenes measure Earth's circumference?
options: By comparing shadow angles between two cities
